executiveSummary: Core Flutter code is substantial and compiles for Android and iOS.
The current repo is not production ready because release signing, store metadata, backend wiring, monetization wiring, privacy links, and real-device WhatsApp verification are incomplete.
Android local distribution is ready via the split arm64 release APK.
iOS local over-the-air distribution is blocked until Apple signing assets and provisioning profiles are added.

verification.name: flutter analyze
verification.details: Completed with 194 issues; mostly warnings and info, but still not clean enough for a production gate.

verification.name: flutter test
verification.details: 974 tests passed locally. This is strong coverage for logic and widgets, but it is not equivalent to real-device validation.

verification.name: flutter build appbundle --release
verification.details: Release Android App Bundle built successfully.

verification.name: flutter build apk --release --split-per-abi
verification.details: Installable split APKs built successfully; arm64 is the preferred local Android artifact.

verification.name: flutter build ios --release --no-codesign
verification.details: iOS Runner.app built successfully without signing. App Store or OTA distribution still requires Apple certificates, provisioning, and export.

artifacts.label: Android arm64 release APK
artifacts.notes: Best artifact for local Wi-Fi download to a modern Android phone.

artifacts.label: Android armeabi-v7a release APK
artifacts.notes: Useful only for older 32-bit Android devices.

artifacts.label: Android universal release APK
artifacts.notes: Very large because it bundles all ABIs and FFmpeg native libraries.

artifacts.label: Android release AAB
artifacts.notes: Primary Play Store upload artifact after signing and store setup are finalized.

artifacts.label: iOS Runner.app
artifacts.notes: Compiled app bundle only. Not directly installable on iPhone without signing/export.

artifacts.label: iOS IPA
artifacts.notes: Will appear only after signed IPA export is added.

features.name: Core app shell, navigation, onboarding, local pack storage
features.evidence: Compiled successfully and covered by widget/unit tests.

features.name: Static sticker editing and WhatsApp image normalization
features.evidence: Covered by unit tests and release builds.

features.name: Animated sticker and video-to-sticker flows
features.evidence: Extensive unit/widget coverage exists, but no recent physical-device run was performed in this audit.

features.name: Shared sticker import from Android share sheet
features.evidence: Native Android intent/import code exists and compiles; needs a real Android phone test.

features.name: WhatsApp Android native sticker pack export
features.evidence: ContentProvider, whitelist checks, and add-pack flow compile and tests cover conversion logic; real WhatsApp verification is still required.

features.name: WhatsApp iOS export
features.evidence: Current app falls back to share sheet on iOS; it is not the same seamless native experience as Android.

features.name: AI sticker generation
features.evidence: Cloud function still returns placeholder data and the Flutter app startup still has Firebase TODO wiring.

features.name: Auth, social feed, search, challenges, profile
features.evidence: UI and local/demo data exist, but Firebase-backed production wiring is incomplete.

features.name: Monetization
features.evidence: AdMob uses test app IDs and RevenueCat is listed in README but not implemented in the app.

blockers.title: Release signing is not production configured
blockers.details: Android release builds are currently signed with the debug key. iOS signing/export is not configured.

blockers.title: Firebase production wiring is incomplete
blockers.details: Firebase packages are commented out in pubspec.yaml and lib/main.dart still contains a Firebase initialization TODO.

blockers.title: AI backend is stubbed
blockers.details: functions/src/index.ts generateSticker still returns placeholder images and does not enforce a real quota pipeline.

blockers.title: Store and compliance metadata are incomplete
blockers.details: StickerContentProvider still serves example privacy/license URLs, support/contact data is missing, and store listing assets have not been automated yet.

blockers.title: Ads and monetization are not production safe
blockers.details: Both Android and iOS still use Google test AdMob app IDs.

blockers.title: No real-device WhatsApp sign-off was performed in this audit
blockers.details: The current machine had no Android phone or iPhone attached, so WhatsApp add-pack flow was not executed against a physical device.

blockers.title: iOS WhatsApp experience is inherently weaker than Android
blockers.details: Current implementation is share-sheet based on iOS. The official WhatsApp sample warns that iOS apps that only export stickers are likely to struggle with App Store review.

blockers.title: Binary size is heavy
blockers.details: arm64 APK is about 107.6MB and universal APK is about 326.6MB, largely because FFmpeg native libraries are bundled for multiple ABIs.

blockers.title: Observability and release telemetry are missing
blockers.details: Crashlytics, Analytics, Remote Config, and App Check are not wired.

blockers.title: Static analysis gate is not clean

items: Configure production Android signing and Play App Signing upload key.
Configure Apple Developer signing, provisioning, and export so a signed IPA can be produced.
Enable Firebase packages, initialize Firebase in Flutter, and wire Auth/Firestore/Storage/Functions end to end.
Replace generateSticker placeholder implementation with the real inference and quota pipeline.
Replace AdMob test IDs and add production monetization wiring.
Replace example privacy/license URLs and add support/contact pages.
Run full Android manual test on a real phone with WhatsApp installed.
Verify sticker import from share sheet, pack creation, tray icon generation, and add-to-WhatsApp flow.
Run full iPhone manual test for editing, export/share, sign-in, and backend-connected flows.
Capture crash logs, performance notes, and broken-edge cases.
Add automated screenshot flows for Android and iOS.
Generate Play Store and App Store screenshots in the required device families and locales.
Create fastlane lanes or equivalent CI for screenshots, metadata, and store upload.
Prepare privacy policy, support site, Data safety form, content ratings, and review notes.
Android: use Internal App Sharing or internal testing after signing is finalized.
iOS: use TestFlight or signed ad hoc OTA once certificates/profiles are available.
Keep the local LAN dashboard for same-Wi-Fi Android installs during development.
Create final release candidates.
Upload store metadata, screenshots, and binaries.
Complete app review notes with demo credentials and reviewer instructions.
Submit Google Play first, then App Store after iOS review-risk items are reduced.

recommendedStack: Fastlane for upload and metadata delivery
Maestro for deterministic cross-platform UI flows and screenshot capture orchestration
Flutter Screenshots or a Maestro-driven Flutter flow for screenshot generation
Optional iOS-only AppScreenshotKit if you want marketing-grade SwiftUI screenshot generation for native iOS views
notes: For this Flutter app, the most practical path is Maestro plus Fastlane, with screenshot export landing directly in the folders that deliver and supply expect.
For Android local testing, serve the split arm64 APK over LAN from this dashboard.
For iOS LAN installation, add signed IPA export and OTA manifest generation later.
